other_observers: Peter Gent saw it alone an hour before we saw it. Others who saw it with me were David Waltman, Randy Seibert, Joanie Kleypas and Tom Delaney.; name unknown--ask Schmoker
specific_location: north side of Hanover Street, between 44th and 45th
description: The bird was the size and shape of a Bullock's oriole, with an entirely black head and back and a black bib coming halfway down the front of the bird, to a rough rounded border with the bright yellow of the rest of the underparts. The wings were mostly black, with a conspicuous yellow patch at the shoulder (lesser coverts) and a single thin white wingbar below that. I did not see any other white on the flight feathers at all. The tail was mostly black, with yellow patches at the base on each side. When the bird flew away from me, I could see a yellow rump.Behaviors: The bird was quite active, moving from treetop to treetop in the neighborhood, coming down to perch on low junipers at least two different times, and once visiting a hanging seed feeder.Call: the bird was silentPlumage: adult
